cards.back: Data that is divided into distinct groups or categories, where each value is a label or description

cards.front: What is the difference between categorical and numerical data?
cards.back: Categorical data is non-numerical, while numerical data can take on any value within a range
Categorical data can be encoded using methods such as one-hot encoding, label encoding, and ordinal encoding
A method of encoding categorical data where each category is represented by a binary vector

cards.front: What is the purpose of exploratory data analysis in categorical data?
cards.back: To understand the distribution and patterns of categorical data, and to identify potential relationships and correlations
